intro: Meineckia parvifolia, also known as African boxwood, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. Traditionally, Meineckia parvifolia has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, diabetes, and inflammation, due to its reported anti-hypertensive, anti-diabetic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Meineckia parvifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Meineckia parvifolia is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
